sentences.quote: We report a Lean~4 formalization that places the sterile-neutrino right-handed Weyl field $\SterileNu$ as a $\mathbb{Z}_2$-graded rung on the same fermionic substrate that hosts the scalar (Higgs) and tetrad condensates of our Phase 5z program (Wave 1, \texttt{ScalarRungInterpretation.lean}) and Phase 4y program (\texttt{TetradGapEquation.lean}).
sentences.quote_normalized: we report a lean~4 formalization that places the sterile-neutrino right-handed weyl field $\sterilenu$ as a $\mathbb{z}_2$-graded rung on the same fermionic substrate that hosts the scalar (higgs) and tetrad condensates of our phase 5z program (wave 1, scalarrunginterpretation.lean) and phase 4y program (tetradgapequation.lean).
sentences.agent_notes: Meta description; module file references resolve.

sentences.quote: The new module \texttt{MajoranaRung.lean} ($14$ substantive theorems, zero sorry, zero new axioms) realizes the \emph{Embedding-III hybrid} construction recommended by our deep-research scoping report.
sentences.quote_normalized: the new module majoranarung.lean ($14$ substantive theorems, zero sorry, zero new axioms) realizes the embedding-iii hybrid construction recommended by our deep-research scoping report.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: abstract states '14 substantive theorems' for MajoranaRung.lean but Section VII Table I (line 414) states '19'. lean_deps.json (excl mk.inj/mk.injEq/mk.sizeOf_spec) = 17; if structure-projection theorems excluded = 14. Internal IA conflict. TN (next sentence covers it): bare H_MR_FromADWSubstrate is referenced.

sentences.quote: $\SterileNu$ is a fundamental Lean field carrying $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ charge $+1$, and the heavy Majorana mass $\HeavyMaj$ is identified with a $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$-invariant condensate scale of the Akama-Diakonov-Wetterich (ADW) substrate---an identification that no primary source closes, and that we therefore encode as a \emph{tracked Prop hypothesis} (\texttt{H\_MR\_FromADWSubstrate}) rather than a derived theorem.
sentences.quote_normalized: $\sterilenu$ is a fundamental lean field carrying $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ charge $+1$, and the heavy majorana mass $\heavymaj$ is identified with a $\mathbb{z}_{16}$-invariant condensate scale of the akama-diakonov-wetterich (adw) substrate---an identification that no primary source closes, and that we therefore encode as a tracked prop hypothesis (h\_mr\_fromadwsubstrate) rather than a derived theorem.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: H_MR_FromADWSubstrate (bare name) NOT in lean_deps.json under MajoranaRung; only H_MR_FromADWSubstrate_BCS_LNV exists. Wave 2b cleanup canonicalized to the BCS_LNV-conjunction form per memory note.

sentences.quote: The $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ singlet-branch bridge to our prior \texttt{HiddenSectorClassification.lean} and \texttt{Z16AnomalyComputation.lean} infrastructure is proved directly: three fundamental $\SterileNu$ Weyl fermions saturate the proved $\mathrm{SM}_{\rm w/o\nu_R}$ deficit $45 \equiv -3 \pmod{16}$ to reach $48 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$, matching observed anomaly cancellation without a hidden TQFT/CFT sector.
sentences.quote_normalized: the $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ singlet-branch bridge to our prior hiddensectorclassification.lean and z16anomalycomputation.lean infrastructure is proved directly: three fundamental $\sterilenu$ weyl fermions saturate the proved $sm_{\rm w/o\nu_r}$ deficit $45 \equiv -3 \pmod{16}$ to reach $48 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$, matching observed anomaly cancellation without a hidden tqft/cft sector.
sentences.agent_notes: Z16 arithmetic: 45 mod 16 = 13 ≡ -3 mod 16; 48 = 16*3 ≡ 0. Bridge theorem majorana_rung_compatible_with_hidden_singlet exists (cites three_singlets_satisfy_hidden_sector + three_nu_R_cancel_three_gen).

sentences.quote: The Type-I seesaw mass relation $\LightNu = y^2 v^2 / \HeavyMaj$ is formalized algebraically with a strict-monotonicity-in-$\HeavyMaj$ theorem and a falsifiability-anchor predicate \texttt{IsObservedSeesawMatch}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the type-i seesaw mass relation $\lightnu = y^2 v^2 / \heavymaj$ is formalized algebraically with a strict-monotonicity-in-$\heavymaj$ theorem and a falsifiability-anchor predicate isobservedseesawmatch.
sentences.agent_notes: seesawNeutrinoMass_strictMono_inv_M_R + IsObservedSeesawMatch present in lean_deps.json.

sentences.quote: A companion module \texttt{NeutrinoMixing.lean} ($7$ theorems) provides a HepLean-style structure note for the PMNS matrix as a $3\!\times\!3$ unitary on $\mathbb{C}$, with a tracked hypothesis $H\_{\rm PMNSAnglesFromSubstrate}$ for the open $\theta_{23} \approx \pi/4$-from-substrate-symmetry conjecture.
sentences.quote_normalized: a companion module neutrinomixing.lean ($7$ theorems) provides a heplean-style structure note for the pmns matrix as a $3\!\times\!3$ unitary on $\mathbb{c}$, with a tracked hypothesis $h\_{\rm pmnsanglesfromsubstrate}$ for the open $\theta_{23} \approx \pi/4$-from-substrate-symmetry conjecture.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: abstract states '7 theorems' but Section VII Table I (line 415) states '11'. lean_deps.json substantive count for NeutrinoMixing = 11. TN: H_PMNSAnglesFromSubstrate (bare) NOT in lean_deps.json; Wave 2a split into H_PMNSAnglesFromExactSubstrate and H_PMNSAnglesFromSubstrate_eps.

sentences.quote: We report two embedding-agnostic phenomenological figures: the seesaw $(y, \HeavyMaj)$-band against NuFit-6.0 oscillation anchors, and the $\mbb$-vs-$m_{\rm lightest}$ "lobster plot" for $0\nu\beta\beta$ against the current KamLAND-Zen 800 bound and the projected LEGEND-1000 reach.
sentences.quote_normalized: we report two embedding-agnostic phenomenological figures: the seesaw $(y, \heavymaj)$-band against nufit-6.0 oscillation anchors, and the $\mbb$-vs-$m_{\rm lightest}$ "lobster plot" for $0\nu\beta\beta$ against the current kamland-zen 800 bound and the projected legend-1000 reach.
sentences.agent_notes: Both figures present on disk (figures/fig_seesaw_y_m_r_band.png + fig_m_beta_beta_vs_m_lightest.png). All 3 citations in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: We surface five honest $WAVE2$-$OPEN$ flags for which no primary literature closes the derivation.
sentences.quote_normalized: we surface five honest $wave2$-$open$ flags for which no primary literature closes the derivation.
sentences.agent_notes: Section IX enumerates O-1..O-5.

sentences.quote: The $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ bridge theorem is the load-bearing formal-verification deliverable; the seesaw and PMNS structure notes lift the surrounding infrastructure to the same machine-checked standard.
sentences.quote_normalized: the $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ bridge theorem is the load-bearing formal-verification deliverable; the seesaw and pmns structure notes lift the surrounding infrastructure to the same machine-checked standard.
sentences.agent_notes: Soft framing claim consistent with main body.

sentences.quote: The Standard Model with three generations is anomaly-free under all local symmetries but acquires a global $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ ($\mathrm{Pin}^+$ Dai-Freed) anomaly when $\nu_R$ is absent: each generation contributes $+15$ Weyl components, summing to $45 \equiv -3 \pmod{16}$ over three generations~\cite{GarciaEtxebarriaMontero2019,WanWang2020}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the standard model with three generations is anomaly-free under all local symmetries but acquires a global $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ ($pin^+$ dai-freed) anomaly when $\nu_r$ is absent: each generation contributes $+15$ weyl components, summing to $45 \equiv -3 \pmod{16}$ over three generations~.
sentences.agent_notes: 3*15=45; 45 mod 16 = 13 ≡ -3 mod 16. Both citations in CITATION_REGISTRY (doi_verified=True).

sentences.quote: Anomaly cancellation requires either three additional $\nu_R$ Weyl fermions (each carrying $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ charge $+1$, restoring $48 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$) or a hidden TQFT/CFT sector saturating the $+3 \pmod{16}$ deficit~\cite{WanWang2020,KawasakiYanagida2023}.
sentences.quote_normalized: anomaly cancellation requires either three additional $\nu_r$ weyl fermions (each carrying $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ charge $+1$, restoring $48 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$) or a hidden tqft/cft sector saturating the $+3 \pmod{16}$ deficit~.
sentences.agent_notes: 48 = 45 + 3 ≡ 0 mod 16. Citations resolve.

sentences.quote: Our prior formalization (\texttt{Z16AnomalyComputation.lean}, \texttt{HiddenSectorClassification.lean}) proved both halves of this ladder in Lean~4: \texttt{three\_nu\_R\_cancel\_three\_gen} and \texttt{three\_singlets\_satisfy\_hidden\_sector}.
sentences.quote_normalized: our prior formalization (z16anomalycomputation.lean, hiddensectorclassification.lean) proved both halves of this ladder in lean~4: three\_nu\_r\_cancel\_three\_gen and three\_singlets\_satisfy\_hidden\_sector.
sentences.agent_notes: Both theorems present in lean_deps.json (top-level SKEFTHawking namespace).

sentences.quote: The sterile-neutrino Type-I seesaw, in turn, generates the observed light-neutrino mass scale $\LightNu \sim 0.05$~eV via $\LightNu = y^2 v^2 / \HeavyMaj$ with $\HeavyMaj$ in the $10^9$--$10^{15}$~GeV range~\cite{MohapatraSmirnov2006}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the sterile-neutrino type-i seesaw, in turn, generates the observed light-neutrino mass scale $\lightnu \sim 0.05$~ev via $\lightnu = y^2 v^2 / \heavymaj$ with $\heavymaj$ in the $10^9$--$10^{15}$~gev range~.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard Type-I seesaw; consistent with NuFit-6 m3≈0.05 eV anchor.

sentences.quote: Within the Akama-Diakonov-Wetterich substrate framework that hosts the tetrad gap equation of Phase~3-4 (\texttt{TetradGapEquation.lean}, \texttt{ADWMechanism.lean}), $\HeavyMaj$ admits a natural identification with the $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$-invariant condensate scale $\LambdaADW$.
sentences.quote_normalized: within the akama-diakonov-wetterich substrate framework that hosts the tetrad gap equation of phase~3-4 (tetradgapequation.lean, adwmechanism.lean), $\heavymaj$ admits a natural identification with the $\mathbb{z}_{16}$-invariant condensate scale $\lambdaadw$.
sentences.agent_notes: Substrate identification claim; encoded as tracked hypothesis (acknowledged next sentence).

sentences.quote: However, no primary source in the ADW~\cite{ADW}, Wetterich-spinor-gravity~\cite{WetterichSpinor2013,WetterichSpinor2022}, or Volovik fermionic-quartet~\cite{Volovik2024Spinor} literature closes the derivation $\LambdaADW \to \HeavyMaj$.
sentences.quote_normalized: however, no primary source in the adw~, wetterich-spinor-gravity~, or volovik fermionic-quartet~ literature closes the derivation $\lambdaadw \to \heavymaj$.
sentences.agent_notes: All four citations resolve in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: This work formalizes the Wave-2 deliverables of the Phase~5z roadmap: the Majorana rung on the same fermionic substrate as the Higgs and tetrad rungs, plus a structure-note PMNS module.
sentences.quote_normalized: this work formalizes the wave-2 deliverables of the phase~5z roadmap: the majorana rung on the same fermionic substrate as the higgs and tetrad rungs, plus a structure-note pmns module.

sentences.quote: We adopt the \emph{Embedding-III hybrid} prescription recommended by our deep-research scoping report: $\SterileNu$ is a fundamental Lean field whose UV interpretation as an ADW-substrate bound state acts only on amplitudes and not on the Lean type signature.
sentences.quote_normalized: we adopt the embedding-iii hybrid prescription recommended by our deep-research scoping report: $\sterilenu$ is a fundamental lean field whose uv interpretation as an adw-substrate bound state acts only on amplitudes and not on the lean type signature.

sentences.quote: The $\HeavyMaj = \LambdaADW$ identification is an explicit \emph{tracked Prop hypothesis}, not a theorem.
sentences.quote_normalized: the $\heavymaj = \lambdaadw$ identification is an explicit tracked prop hypothesis, not a theorem.
sentences.agent_notes: Hypothesis disclosure: H_MR_FromADWSubstrate_BCS_LNV (Wave 2b strengthened).

sentences.quote_normalized: the sterile-neutrino structure is encoded as [verbatim block: structure sterileneutrino where generation : fin 3; def z16charge : zmod 16 := 1].

sentences.quote: Each instance carries $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ charge $+1$; the assignment is locked by the proved Garcia-Etxebarria-Montero tower~\cite{GarciaEtxebarriaMontero2019} and the project-existing \texttt{Z16AnomalyComputation.three\_nu\_R\_cancel\_three\_gen} ($48 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$).
sentences.quote_normalized: each instance carries $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ charge $+1$; the assignment is locked by the proved garcia-etxebarria-montero tower~ and the project-existing z16anomalycomputation.three\_nu\_r\_cancel\_three\_gen ($48 \equiv 0 \pmod{16}$).

sentences.agent_notes: All three theorems exist in lean_deps.json.

sentences.quote: This is the formal statement that Embedding III adds zero IR cost relative to the with-$\nu_R$ branch: three fundamental $\SterileNu$ Weyl saturate the SM-without-$\nu_R$ deficit, with no separate hidden TQFT/CFT content required~\cite{WanWang2020}.
sentences.quote_normalized: this is the formal statement that embedding iii adds zero ir cost relative to the with-$\nu_r$ branch: three fundamental $\sterilenu$ weyl saturate the sm-without-$\nu_r$ deficit, with no separate hidden tqft/cft content required~.

sentences.quote: The Wan-Wang Ultra-Unification ladder admits the alternative "Embedding II" branch in which $\nu_R$ is absent and the deficit is saturated by an SM-singlet TQFT plus fermionic vortex zero-mode condensation~\cite{WanWang2020}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the wan-wang ultra-unification ladder admits the alternative "embedding ii" branch in which $\nu_r$ is absent and the deficit is saturated by an sm-singlet tqft plus fermionic vortex zero-mode condensation~.

sentences.quote: That branch is not formalized in this work; the deep-research scoping report finds no primary source that closes the analogous bridge to a substrate condensate.
sentences.quote_normalized: that branch is not formalized in this work; the deep-research scoping report finds no primary source that closes the analogous bridge to a substrate condensate.

sentences.quote_normalized: heavy majorana mass data is bundled in [verbatim: structure majoranarungdata where m_r: fin 3 -> r, m_r_pos].

sentences.quote: The Type-I seesaw light-neutrino mass is the noncomputable definition $\LightNu = y^2 v^2 / \HeavyMaj$, formalized as \texttt{seesawNeutrinoMass} with five algebraic theorems: non-negativity (any real $y, v$); strict positivity for $y, v \neq 0$; zero-iff-Yukawa-or-VEV-vanishes; and the Type-I-seesaw-signature strict monotonicity.
sentences.quote_normalized: the type-i seesaw light-neutrino mass is the noncomputable definition $\lightnu = y^2 v^2 / \heavymaj$, formalized as seesawneutrinomass with five algebraic theorems: non-negativity (any real $y, v$); strict positivity for $y, v \neq 0$; zero-iff-yukawa-or-vev-vanishes; and the type-i-seesaw-signature strict monotonicity.
sentences.agent_notes: seesawNeutrinoMass + 5 algebraic theorems (def/_nonneg/_pos_of_nonzero/_zero_iff/_strictMono_inv_M_R) all in lean_deps.json.

sentences.quote: (at fixed nonzero $y, v$). The latter is the only structurally distinctive seesaw consequence (heavy mass suppresses light mass); all five theorems are direct algebraic consequences of $M_R > 0$.
sentences.quote_normalized: (at fixed nonzero $y, v$). the latter is the only structurally distinctive seesaw consequence (heavy mass suppresses light mass); all five theorems are direct algebraic consequences of $m_r > 0$.

sentences.quote_normalized: figure~ shows the (y, $\heavymaj$) plane with nufit-6.0~ oscillation anchors.

sentences.quote: [Figure caption fig:seesaw-y-m-r-band] Type-I seesaw $(y, \HeavyMaj)$-plane against NuFit-6.0 oscillation anchors. The shaded band marks the natural-seesaw region between the $m_3 \approx 0.05$~eV atmospheric contour and the $\LightNu \lesssim 0.1$~eV cosmology cap. The star marks the canonical Type-I anchor $(y_{\rm top}, \LambdaADW \!\sim\! 10^{16}~{\rm GeV})$; the diamond marks the Wave-2 lower-scan anchor. Lean reference: \texttt{MajoranaRung.seesawNeutrinoMass\_strictMono\_inv\_M\_R}.

sentences.agent_notes: Lean ref resolves; figure file present.

sentences.quote: A focussed Wave 2a deep-research return on channel projection of the NJL/Wetterich gap equation onto the lepton-Majorana sector~\cite{AntuschKerstenLindnerRatz2003,Hill2024Bilocal} confirms that no primary source closes $\LambdaADW \to \HeavyMaj$, and surfaces a clean \emph{structural obstruction theorem} rooted in lepton-number symmetry.
sentences.quote_normalized: a focussed wave 2a deep-research return on channel projection of the njl/wetterich gap equation onto the lepton-majorana sector~ confirms that no primary source closes $\lambdaadw \to \heavymaj$, and surfaces a clean structural obstruction theorem rooted in lepton-number symmetry.

sentences.quote:
The Majorana bilinear $
u_R^T C 
u_R$ carries $\Delta L = 2$; if the substrate preserves $U(1)_L$ (or its discrete remnant $\mathbb{Z}_{4,X}$), the four-fermion Majorana-channel coupling $G_M (
u_R^T C 
u_R)(
u_R^T C 
u_R)^*$ is forbidden by symmetry.
sentences.quote_normalized:
the majorana bilinear $
u_r^t c 
u_r$ carries $\delta l = 2$; if the substrate preserves $u(1)_l$ (or its discrete remnant $\mathbb{z}_{4,x}$), the four-fermion majorana-channel coupling $g_m (
u_r^t c 
u_r)(
u_r^t c 
u_r)^*$ is forbidden by symmetry.

sentences.quote: Antusch-Kersten-Lindner-Ratz~\cite{AntuschKerstenLindnerRatz2003} state this explicitly: ``We have included huge Majorana masses for the right-handed neutrinos, since there is no protective symmetry.''
sentences.quote_normalized: antusch-kersten-lindner-ratz~ state this explicitly: ``we have included huge majorana masses for the right-handed neutrinos, since there is no protective symmetry.''

sentences.quote: The contrapositive is the obstruction: under substrate-$L$ preservation, $G_M \equiv 0$ and the projected gap equation has only $\HeavyMaj = 0$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the contrapositive is the obstruction: under substrate-$l$ preservation, $g_m \equiv 0$ and the projected gap equation has only $\heavymaj = 0$.

sentences.quote: When the substrate explicitly violates $U(1)_L$, the qualitative form of $\HeavyMaj$ is the standard NJL/BCS \emph{dimensional-transmutation} exponential~\cite{AntuschKerstenLindnerRatz2003}, formalized as the strong tracked hypothesis \texttt{MajoranaRung.H\_MR\_FromADWSubstrate\_BCS\_LNV} [verbatim block].
sentences.quote_normalized: when the substrate explicitly violates $u(1)_l$, the qualitative form of $\heavymaj$ is the standard njl/bcs dimensional-transmutation exponential~, formalized as the strong tracked hypothesis majoranarung.h\_mr\_fromadwsubstrate\_bcs\_lnv [verbatim block].
sentences.agent_notes: H_MR_FromADWSubstrate_BCS_LNV present in lean_deps.json.

sentences.quote: The conjunction with \texttt{H\_LeptonNumberViolated} encodes the symmetry obstruction; $G_M > 1$ encodes supercriticality.
sentences.quote_normalized: the conjunction with h\_leptonnumberviolated encodes the symmetry obstruction; $g_m > 1$ encodes supercriticality.

sentences.quote: The Wave 2b Lean module ships the obstruction theorem \texttt{lepton\_number\_symmetry\_obstructs\_BCS\_form} as a direct consequence: \emph{without LNV, the strong BCS hypothesis cannot hold}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the wave 2b lean module ships the obstruction theorem lepton\_number\_symmetry\_obstructs\_bcs\_form as a direct consequence: without lnv, the strong bcs hypothesis cannot hold.

sentences.quote: Two further refinement theorems are shipped: \texttt{M\_R\_pos\_under\_BCS\_form} (the BCS exponential is strictly positive, hence so is $\HeavyMaj$) and \texttt{M\_R\_lt\_substrate\_under\_BCS\_form} (the BCS exponential is strictly bounded above by 1, hence $\HeavyMaj < \LambdaADW$).
sentences.quote_normalized: two further refinement theorems are shipped: m\_r\_pos\_under\_bcs\_form (the bcs exponential is strictly positive, hence so is $\heavymaj$) and m\_r\_lt\_substrate\_under\_bcs\_form (the bcs exponential is strictly bounded above by 1, hence $\heavymaj < \lambdaadw$).

sentences.quote: This Wave 2b strengthening pattern follows the project's tracked-hypothesis precedent~\cite{TooBySmithHepLean}: the \texttt{H\_ScalarChannelIsTetradBifurcationOutput} hypothesis in \texttt{ScalarRungInterpretation.lean}, the \texttt{H\_MixedChannelZ16Cancels} hypothesis in \texttt{HiddenSectorMixedCharge.lean}, and the \texttt{H\_VestigialRelicCarriesZ16Charge} hypothesis in \texttt{DarkSectorSynthesis.lean}.
sentences.quote_normalized: this wave 2b strengthening pattern follows the project's tracked-hypothesis precedent~: the h\_scalarchannelistetradbifurcationoutput hypothesis in scalarrunginterpretation.lean, the h\_mixedchannelz16cancels hypothesis in hiddensectormixedcharge.lean, and the h\_vestigialreliccarriesz16charge hypothesis in darksectorsynthesis.lean.
sentences.agent_notes: All three referenced hypotheses present in lean_deps.json.

sentences.quote: The companion module \texttt{NeutrinoMixing.lean} defines a HepLean-style~\cite{TooBySmithHepLean} PMNS structure together with element accessors, the unitarity identities $\PMNSMatrix^\dagger \PMNSMatrix = \PMNSMatrix \PMNSMatrix^\dagger = \mathbb{1}$, and the identity-matrix non-emptiness witness.
sentences.quote_normalized: the companion module neutrinomixing.lean defines a heplean-style~ pmns structure together with element accessors, the unitarity identities $\pmnsmatrix^\dagger \pmnsmatrix = \pmnsmatrix \pmnsmatrix^\dagger = \mathbb{1}$, and the identity-matrix non-emptiness witness.

sentences.quote: The Dirac-vs-Majorana physical interpretation is exposed as parametric \texttt{Prop} markers \texttt{IsDiracPMNS} and \texttt{IsMajoranaPMNS}; the PMNS matrix structure is identical in both cases, but the right-hand diagonal phases (two Majorana phases $\alpha_1, \alpha_2$) are physical observables in the Majorana case and unphysical phase-rephasings in the Dirac case.
sentences.quote_normalized: the dirac-vs-majorana physical interpretation is exposed as parametric prop markers isdiracpmns and ismajoranapmns; the pmns matrix structure is identical in both cases, but the right-hand diagonal phases (two majorana phases $\alpha_1, \alpha_2$) are physical observables in the majorana case and unphysical phase-rephasings in the dirac case.

sentences.quote: The full PMNS standard parameterization $U_{\rm PMNS} = R_{23}(\theta_{23}) U_\delta R_{13}(\theta_{13}) U_\delta^{-1} R_{12}(\theta_{12}) \cdot \mathrm{diag}(e^{i\alpha_1/2}, e^{i\alpha_2/2}, 1)$ is provided as a Python implementation (\texttt{src/core/formulas.py:pmns\_unitary\_matrix}) and validated against $|U_{e3}|^2 = \sin^2\theta_{13}$ and unitarity to machine precision.
sentences.quote_normalized: the full pmns standard parameterization $u_{\rm pmns} = r_{23}(\theta_{23}) u_\delta r_{13}(\theta_{13}) u_\delta^{-1} r_{12}(\theta_{12}) \cdot diag(e^{i\alpha_1/2}, e^{i\alpha_2/2}, 1)$ is provided as a python implementation (src/core/formulas.py:pmns\_unitary\_matrix) and validated against $|u_{e3}|^2 = \sin^2\theta_{13}$ and unitarity to machine precision.

sentences.quote: No primary source closes the derivation~\cite{Volovik2024Spinor}.
sentences.quote_normalized: no primary source closes the derivation~.

sentences.quote: The effective Majorana mass probed by neutrinoless double-beta decay is the embedding-agnostic combination $\mbb = \left| \sum_{i=1}^{3} U_{ei}^2 \, m_i \right|$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the effective majorana mass probed by neutrinoless double-beta decay is the embedding-agnostic combination $\mbb = \left| \sum_{i=1}^{3} u_{ei}^2 \, m_i \right|$.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard 0νββ definition.

sentences.quote: Figure~\ref{fig:m-bb-vs-m-lightest} shows the standard "lobster plot" of $\mbb$ against the lightest neutrino mass $m_{\rm lightest}$, swept over the full range of Majorana phases at NuFit-6.0~\cite{NuFit60} best-fit angles.
sentences.quote_normalized: figure~ shows the standard "lobster plot" of $\mbb$ against the lightest neutrino mass $m_{\rm lightest}$, swept over the full range of majorana phases at nufit-6.0~ best-fit angles.

sentences.quote: Both normal ordering (NO) and inverted ordering (IO) bands are shown.
sentences.quote_normalized: both normal ordering (no) and inverted ordering (io) bands are shown.

sentences.quote: [Figure caption fig:m-bb-vs-m-lightest] Effective Majorana mass $\mbb$ vs lightest neutrino mass; NO and IO bands at NuFit-6.0 best-fit; KamLAND-Zen 800 90% CL bound (28-122 meV; NME spread); LEGEND-1000 99.7% CL projected reach (9-21 meV); IO fully discoverable; NO largely out of reach; embedding-agnostic. Lean reference: \texttt{NeutrinoMixing.PMNSMatrix}.
sentences.quote_normalized: [figure caption fig:m-bb-vs-m-lightest] effective majorana mass $\mbb$ vs lightest neutrino mass; no and io bands at nufit-6.0 best-fit; kamland-zen 800 90% cl bound (28-122 mev; nme spread); legend-1000 99.7% cl projected reach (9-21 mev); io fully discoverable; no largely out of reach; embedding-agnostic. lean reference: neutrinomixing.pmnsmatrix.
sentences.agent_notes: Bounds match primary sources (KamLAND-Zen 2406.11438; LEGEND-1000 2107.11462). Lean ref resolves.

sentences.quote: The conclusions are: (i)~the IO band lies entirely within the LEGEND-1000 reach; (ii)~the NO band is largely out of reach and $\mbb$ provides essentially no embedding discrimination; (iii)~the KamLAND-Zen 800 bound has already excluded the quasi-degenerate (QD) hierarchy region.
sentences.quote_normalized: the conclusions are: (i)~the io band lies entirely within the legend-1000 reach; (ii)~the no band is largely out of reach and $\mbb$ provides essentially no embedding discrimination; (iii)~the kamland-zen 800 bound has already excluded the quasi-degenerate (qd) hierarchy region.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard 0νββ phenomenology consensus.

sentences.quote: There is no embedding-distinguishing $\mbb$ prediction in any current ADW/Volovik or Wetterich-spinor-gravity source~\cite{ADW,WetterichSpinor2013,Volovik2024Spinor} (deep research \S 4); the figure encodes the embedding-agnostic phenomenology only.
sentences.quote_normalized: there is no embedding-distinguishing $\mbb$ prediction in any current adw/volovik or wetterich-spinor-gravity source~ (deep research \s 4); the figure encodes the embedding-agnostic phenomenology only.

sentences.quote: A Wave 2a deep-research return on the IR-equivalence of Embedding I (fundamental $\SterileNu$) and Embedding III (substrate-bound $\SterileNu$) confirms that the Appelquist-Carazzone decoupling theorem~\cite{AppelquistCarazzone1975,BallThorne1994} applies cleanly, with leading operator dimension $\Delta = 5$ (Weinberg-like, LNV) or $\Delta = 6$ (generic), giving the schematic bound $|\mathrm{amp}_{\rm III} - \mathrm{amp}_{\rm I}| \le C \cdot (E / \LambdaADW)^k, k \in \{1, 2\}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: a wave 2a deep-research return on the ir-equivalence of embedding i (fundamental $\sterilenu$) and embedding iii (substrate-bound $\sterilenu$) confirms that the appelquist-carazzone decoupling theorem~ applies cleanly, with leading operator dimension $\delta = 5$ (weinberg-like, lnv) or $\delta = 6$ (generic), giving the schematic bound $|amp_{\rm iii} - amp_{\rm i}| \le c \cdot (e / \lambdaadw)^k, k \in \{1, 2\}$.

sentences.quote: The natural Wilson-coefficient estimate is $C \sim N_f/(16\pi^2) \simeq 0.1$ for ADW-style substrates with $N_f \simeq 16$, transplanted from the SILH counting of Giudice-Grojean-Pomarol-Rattazzi~\cite{GiudiceGrojeanPomarolRattazzi2007} and the bilocal-NJL form-factor analysis of Hill~\cite{Hill2024Bilocal}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the natural wilson-coefficient estimate is $c \sim n_f/(16\pi^2) \simeq 0.1$ for adw-style substrates with $n_f \simeq 16$, transplanted from the silh counting of giudice-grojean-pomarol-rattazzi~ and the bilocal-njl form-factor analysis of hill~.

sentences.quote: The substrate-specific value of $C$ has not been computed in primary literature for ADW; we honestly flag this as the residual gap.
sentences.quote_normalized: the substrate-specific value of $c$ has not been computed in primary literature for adw; we honestly flag this as the residual gap.

sentences.quote: A \texttt{DecouplingRegime} structure encoding $0 < E$, $0 < \LambdaADW$, $10 E < \LambdaADW$, plus three predicates excluding cosmological-scale failure modes (vestigial gravity~\cite{Volovik2024Spinor}; q-theory; FRG strong-coupling). At $E = M_W$ and $\LambdaADW = 10^{14}$~GeV, all three failure-mode predicates are trivially satisfied.
sentences.quote_normalized: a decouplingregime structure encoding $0 < e$, $0 < \lambdaadw$, $10 e < \lambdaadw$, plus three predicates excluding cosmological-scale failure modes (vestigial gravity~; q-theory; frg strong-coupling). at $e = m_w$ and $\lambdaadw = 10^{14}$~gev, all three failure-mode predicates are trivially satisfied.
sentences.agent_notes: DecouplingRegime structure with 6 fields all in lean_deps.json. M_W ≈ 80 GeV, Λ=1e14 GeV → ratio 8e-13 << threshold.

sentences.quote: A \texttt{SubstrateData} structure parameterizing the substrate by $(\LambdaADW, \Lambda_{\rm UV}, N_f)$.
sentences.quote_normalized: a substratedata structure parameterizing the substrate by $(\lambdaadw, \lambda_{\rm uv}, n_f)$.

sentences.quote: Two tracked-hypothesis Props: \texttt{H\_DecouplingBoundDim6} (generic, $k=2$) and \texttt{H\_DecouplingBoundDim5\_LNV} (LNV-conditional, $k=1$, dim-5 Weinberg-operator channel).
sentences.quote_normalized: two tracked-hypothesis props: h\_decouplingbounddim6 (generic, $k=2$) and h\_decouplingbounddim5\_lnv (lnv-conditional, $k=1$, dim-5 weinberg-operator channel).

sentences.quote: A non-vacuity witness \texttt{decouplingRegime\_at\_electroweak\_scale} showing the canonical EW point $(E, \LambdaADW) = (80~\mathrm{GeV}, 10^{14}~\mathrm{GeV})$ satisfies the regime predicate.
sentences.quote_normalized: a non-vacuity witness decouplingregime\_at\_electroweak\_scale showing the canonical ew point $(e, \lambdaadw) = (80~gev, 10^{14}~gev)$ satisfies the regime predicate.

sentences.quote: A consistency witness \texttt{H\_DecouplingBoundDim6\_consistent} showing the trivially-zero amplitude difference satisfies the bound for any \texttt{SubstrateData}.
sentences.quote_normalized: a consistency witness h\_decouplingbounddim6\_consistent showing the trivially-zero amplitude difference satisfies the bound for any substratedata.

sentences.quote: A \texttt{decoupling\_isBigOWith} restatement using Mathlib's \texttt{Asymptotics.IsBigOWith} API for compatibility with downstream asymptotic-analysis infrastructure.
sentences.quote_normalized: a decoupling\_isbigowith restatement using mathlib's asymptotics.isbigowith api for compatibility with downstream asymptotic-analysis infrastructure.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: bare name decoupling_isBigOWith NOT in lean_deps.json. Actual name is H_DecouplingBoundDim6_isBigOWith. Likely shorthand drift in paper.

sentences.quote: For $E = M_W \simeq 80$~GeV and $\LambdaADW = 10^{14}$~GeV, the leading dim-5 (LNV) suppression is $(E/\LambdaADW) \simeq 8 \times 10^{-13}$, corresponding to $m_\nu \sim C \cdot v^2 / \LambdaADW \simeq 0.06$~eV (at $C \simeq N_f/(16\pi^2) \simeq 0.1$, default in \texttt{src/core/formulas.py:weinberg\_induced\_neutrino\_mass}) — within the LEGEND-1000~\cite{LEGEND1000} reach.
sentences.quote_normalized: for $e = m_w \simeq 80$~gev and $\lambdaadw = 10^{14}$~gev, the leading dim-5 (lnv) suppression is $(e/\lambdaadw) \simeq 8 \times 10^{-13}$, corresponding to $m_\nu \sim c \cdot v^2 / \lambdaadw \simeq 0.06$~ev (at $c \simeq n_f/(16\pi^2) \simeq 0.1$, default in src/core/formulas.py:weinberg\_induced\_neutrino\_mass) -- within the legend-1000~ reach.
sentences.agent_notes: Verified: 80/1e14 = 8e-13; 0.1*246²/1e14*1e9 = 0.0605 eV ≈ 0.06 eV (delta 0.9%); formulas.py:weinberg_induced_neutrino_mass exists.

sentences.quote: The dim-6 (generic) suppression is $(E/\LambdaADW)^2 \simeq 6 \times 10^{-25}$, far below DUNE/Hyper-K NSI sensitivity~\cite{CiriglianoMasterFormula2018}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the dim-6 (generic) suppression is $(e/\lambdaadw)^2 \simeq 6 \times 10^{-25}$, far below dune/hyper-k nsi sensitivity~.
sentences.agent_notes: (80/1e14)^2 = 6.4e-25 ≈ 6e-25 (delta 6.7% — within 1-sigfig agreement).

sentences.quote: The phenomenological signature of decoupling is therefore the same as ordinary Type-I seesaw — it is the \emph{correct} behavior, not an embedding-distinguishing feature.
sentences.quote_normalized: the phenomenological signature of decoupling is therefore the same as ordinary type-i seesaw -- it is the correct behavior, not an embedding-distinguishing feature.

sentences.quote: An auxiliary lemma \texttt{dim6\_tighter\_than\_dim5\_in\_decoupling\_regime} establishes $(E/\LambdaADW)^2 \le E/\LambdaADW$ in the decoupling regime, confirming that the dim-6 bound is \emph{tighter} (smaller upper bound) than dim-5.
sentences.quote_normalized: an auxiliary lemma dim6\_tighter\_than\_dim5\_in\_decoupling\_regime establishes $(e/\lambdaadw)^2 \le e/\lambdaadw$ in the decoupling regime, confirming that the dim-6 bound is tighter (smaller upper bound) than dim-5.

sentences.quote: The two bounds therefore apply in different physical regimes — dim-6 when the substrate preserves $U(1)_L$ and the dim-5 channel is symmetry-forbidden, dim-5 when the substrate violates $U(1)_L$ and the Weinberg operator is allowed — and are NOT in an implication relation.
sentences.quote_normalized: the two bounds therefore apply in different physical regimes -- dim-6 when the substrate preserves $u(1)_l$ and the dim-5 channel is symmetry-forbidden, dim-5 when the substrate violates $u(1)_l$ and the weinberg operator is allowed -- and are not in an implication relation.

sentences.quote: The two new Lean modules are summarized in Table~\ref{tab:formal-summary}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the two new lean modules are summarized in table~.

sentences.quote: Beyond the load-bearing $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ singlet-branch bridge, the formalization includes: (i)~five seesaw theorems (positivity, nonzero-positivity, zero-iff, monotonicity, defining identity); (ii)~three substrate-bridge theorems (the \texttt{H\_MR\_FromADWSubstrate} tracked hypothesis, $\HeavyMaj \leq \LambdaADW$ under the bridge, and the bridge-falsifiability witness); (iii)~the \texttt{IsObservedSeesawMatch} falsifiability anchor with its $M_R$-too-large witness, mirroring Wave 1's $v$-too-large anchor for the Higgs prediction.
sentences.quote_normalized: beyond the load-bearing $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ singlet-branch bridge, the formalization includes: (i)~five seesaw theorems (positivity, nonzero-positivity, zero-iff, monotonicity, defining identity); (ii)~three substrate-bridge theorems (the h\_mr\_fromadwsubstrate tracked hypothesis, $\heavymaj \leq \lambdaadw$ under the bridge, and the bridge-falsifiability witness); (iii)~the isobservedseesawmatch falsifiability anchor with its $m_r$-too-large witness, mirroring wave 1's $v$-too-large anchor for the higgs prediction.
sentences.agent_notes: TN: H_MR_FromADWSubstrate (bare) NOT in lean_deps.json; only _BCS_LNV variant exists. Other refs OK: IsObservedSeesawMatch ✓, not_isObservedSeesawMatch_of_M_R_too_large ✓, M_R_lt_substrate_under_BCS_form ✓.

sentences.quote: The PMNS module ships seven theorems: two unitarity identities ($\PMNSMatrix^\dagger \PMNSMatrix = \mathbb{1}$ both ways), two identity-PMNS structural lemmas, the \texttt{IsMajoranaPMNS\_of\_majoranaRungData} bridge to the \texttt{MajoranaRung} module, and the \texttt{identity\_does\_not\_satisfy\_substrate\_hypothesis} non-vacuity witness for WAVE2-OPEN-2.

sentences.quote: O-1: $\HeavyMaj = c \cdot \LambdaADW$ for any per-generation coefficient $c \in (0, 1]$. Substrate parameters $(\Lambda_{\rm UV}, N_f, G_c)$ leave $\HeavyMaj$ as a fit parameter in all current sources~\cite{ADW,WetterichSpinor2013,Volovik2024Spinor}. Encoded as \texttt{H\_MR\_FromADWSubstrate}.

sentences.quote_normalized: o-3: majorana phases from composite-operator structure (embedding ii claim). unrealized in any source we located.

blocking_issues.summary: Internal arithmetic / count drift: abstract states '14' MajoranaRung theorems and '7' NeutrinoMixing theorems but Section VII Table I states '19' and '11'; test count claim '32 tests / 6 classes' actual is '40 / 7'; \totalmodules and \totalaxioms LaTeX macros are undefined in counts.tex (should be \leanmodules=170 and \axiomcount=1).

Theorem-name reference drift (Wave 2a/2b stale references): bare names H_MR_FromADWSubstrate (5 occurrences in abstract+body+OPEN-list), H_PMNSAnglesFromSubstrate (3 occurrences), identity_does_not_satisfy_substrate_hypothesis (2 occurrences), and decoupling_isBigOWith (1 occurrence) are absent from lean_deps.json. Live names are H_MR_FromADWSubstrate_BCS_LNV; H_PMNSAnglesFromExactSubstrate / H_PMNSAnglesFromSubstrate_eps; identity_does_not_satisfy_exact_substrate_hypothesis / _eps_substrate_hypothesis; H_DecouplingBoundDim6_isBigOWith. These stale references reflect Wave 2a/2b refactors that did not propagate to paper prose.
